plugin_desc: Wi-Tek managed switch-specific SNMP collection (enterprise PEN 12284). Beyond the shared Switch SNMP floor, it collects Wi-Tek private OIDs from SYSINFO-MIB and IMIMGMT-MIB for device health including control-plane CPU utilization, memory used/free/usage, system temperature, fan state and power-supply state. CPU is read from taskInformation.total as a direct percentage. Memory usage is derived from allocated and free bytes. Temperature is read as degrees Celsius from the solarCfg system temperature scalar. Fan and PSU raw states are normalized through telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. The shared Switch floor supplies uptime, IF-MIB interface metrics, 64-bit ifHCInOctets/ifHCOutOctets and aggregate traffic roll-ups.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports Wi-Tek switch control-plane CPU utilization from SYSINFO-MIB taskInformation.total as a direct 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate management-plane overload or abnormal traffic pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports allocated memory bytes from Wi-Tek SYSINFO-MIB memoryInformation.memAllocBytes.

metrics.name: device_memory_free
metrics.description: This metric reports free memory bytes from Wi-Tek SYSINFO-MIB memoryInformation.memFreeBytes.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric derives Wi-Tek memory utilization from allocated and free memory bytes. High memory usage may indicate oversized forwarding tables, memory leaks or insufficient capacity.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports Wi-Tek system temperature in degrees Celsius from IMIMGMT-MIB solarCfg.systemTemperature. High temperature indicates thermal risk and can precede instability or hardware damage.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports Wi-Tek fan health normalized to 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates fan failure or an unknown fan condition requiring inspection.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports Wi-Tek power-supply health normalized to 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates abnormal AC-DC or battery power state.

metrics.name: device_fan_ac_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the AC power state of the device fan, normalized to 1 (normal) or 2 (abnormal).

metrics.name: device_psu_ac_dc_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the AC/DC power state of the device PSU, normalized to 1 (normal) or 2 (abnormal).

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
